title: Scratch Test
prompt: Which rock is hardest and will scratch the others?
explanation: Granite is very hard with interlocking crystals. Chalk is soft and crumbly.
funFact: Granite is used for kitchen worktops because it resists scratches!

title: Water Drop Test
explanation: Sandstone has tiny gaps between sand grains so water soaks through. Slate and marble are impermeable.
funFact: Roof tiles are often slate because rain runs off not through!

title: Rock Sorter
prompt: Sort rocks by how they were made
explanation: Sedimentary rocks form from layers squashed, igneous from cooled magma, metamorphic changed by heat and pressure.
funFact: Marble is limestone that got cooked deep underground!

title: Fossil Formation
prompt: What is the first step to make a fossil?

options: Animal dies and gets buried quickly in mud

explanation: Quick burial stops rotting. Layers harden to rock around bones or shells over millions of years.
funFact: Mary Anning found amazing fossils on Dorset cliffs when she was only 12!

title: Soil Shake
prompt: After shaking soil in water jar and waiting, which settles at bottom?
explanation: Heaviest sand sinks first, then silt, then light clay on top, humus floats.
funFact: Soil is made from tiny rock bits plus dead plants and animals — humus!

title: Solid Liquid Gas Sort
prompt: Sort these into solid liquid gas
explanation: Solids keep shape, liquids flow and take container shape, gases spread out to fill space.
funFact: You cannot pour a solid but you can pour a liquid — try pouring a chair!

title: Melt Race
explanation: Higher temperature means faster melting. Ice melts at 0 degrees Celsius.
funFact: Foil reflects heat so it actually slows melting — great for ice lollies!

title: Water Cycle Step
prompt: What is it called when water vapour turns to droplets on cold glass?
explanation: Evaporation is liquid to gas when heated. Condensation is gas to liquid when cooled.
funFact: Clouds are billions of tiny condensation droplets floating!

title: Thermometer Read
prompt: Water boils at what temperature in Celsius?
explanation: Water freezes at 0°C and boils at 100°C at sea level. In between it is liquid.
funFact: On Mount Everest water boils at about 71°C because air pressure is lower!
